Colorama Guitars Fretted Americana Inc.
1858 Hofner Colorama (Selmer, London).
This 13-inch-wide, inch-deep, electric semi-solid guitar weighs just 4.10 lbs.
Heavily reinforced laminated plywood back over framed sides with dark Cellulose finish with twin gold stripes around edge body headstock.
body (bass side) Höfner decal gold with black trim.
Two-piece maple neck with beech center strip width just over 11/16 inches, scale length inches wonderful thick 'baseball-bat' profile.
'Slab' rosewood fretboard with three-layer black over white plastic nut, 'zero' original medium frets inlaid pearl markers.
Headstock face with matching twin gold stripes.
Three-in-a-line open-back strip tuners with oval white plastic buttons.
Serial number "71" stamped blind back headstock.
Specific shaped three-layer, black over white plastic pickguard with four screws.
Single Höfner "black-bar" single-coil black plastic 'squared corner' pickup neck position with output 6.00k.
rotary volume control three two-way tone-selector slider switches mounted pickguard.
Höfner metal bar-bridge height-adjustable metal base Höfner basic 'trapeze' tailpiece secured lower edge pins screw.
small surface marks (mainly edges) that prevent this guitar from being mint.
Conservatively, this exceptionally rare little guitar near mint (9.25) condition complete with original brown leather strap fasteners.
Housed original guitar) three-latch, shaped brown cream softshell case with brown felt lining (9.00).
Unlike most early Höfner guitars this action great playability also unique 'rock roll' sound.
This model first Colorama imported into Selmer.
originally started life Germany Hofner Model (single pickup) Model (twin pickup) which were produced European market from 1956 1960.
guitar features "semi-solid" body, looks like solid guitar really heavily reinforced plywood box.
very earliest examples "tear" shaped pickguard, with later models (1959-60) having more stylized shape (like this one).
square cornered version standard Hofner "black-bar" pickup fitted, without usual rotary height adjustment wheels.
guitar featured here original dark cellulose finish with twin gold stripes around edge body headstock full years before Fender used 'racing-stripes' their Mustang).
Selmer list price 1959 eighteen guineas.
(The Vintage Hofner Website).

Judas Priest released British Steel April 1980, there many fans historians would swear their black leather jackets that legendary band game with that propulsive, iconic album.
Wings Destiny, Screaming Vengeance, Defenders Faith Painkiller hold prominent position pantheon excellent important metal releases, when comes influence, consistency accessibility, British Steel perhaps, notch above them all.
Arriving during very outset Wave British Heavy Metal movement, 1980's British Steel forged template countless bands, including Iron Maiden, Saxon, Diamond Head Grim Reaper.
British Steel isn't Judas Priest's most musically complex album.
Most songs simple straightforward, from anti-authoritarian charge "Breaking Law" (the opening track U.S. release) flailing guitar leads rhythmic, pre-thrash barrage "Rapid Fire" (track U.K.) it's complete, satisfying cohesive release.
There's musical experimentation ("Metal Gods," "The Rage"), some chant-along anthems ("United," "You Don't Have Wise") plenty moments custom-made fist-in-the-air headbanging.
From album's immaculate construction natural flow, might guess Judas Priest spent months perfecting songs before tracking them over equal amount time.
actuality, once they entered Tittenhurst Park Recording Studios, live-in estate English country Berkshire, England, that formerly owned John Lennon, band days record with producer Allom.
While Judas Priest were recording British Steel, their management already planning tour.
There margin error when came scheduling, being under provided incentive.
Priest time second-guess themselves, they went with first ideas first takes, which gave album sense immediacy.
great chemistry while Judas Priest worked British Steel, they barely time finish songs alone back analyze what they were creating.
soon they finished tracking, they were onto their next career step, rehearsing tour that scheduled launch March 1980.
would more than month before album came even longer before "Living After Midnight" airwaves fire.
Priest didn't play songs from British Steel tour, which featured opener Iron Maiden.
When they arrived States that summer 1980, they added "Living After Midnight," "Steeler," "You Don't Have Wise" "Grinder" their set.
